mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly compressing forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the positive direction over a 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are positive in >65% of rising rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate above 4.0% signals immediate labor market deterioration, triggering rapid equity sell-offs within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 5-day forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal shows no statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate broad consumer demand collapse, driving negative equity returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint decline of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative 60-day returns with an accuracy above 50%, or the interaction term loses significance when added to a baseline regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth exceeding 2.0% year-over-year during inflationary periods confirms robust supply capacity, supporting positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth above 2.0% fails to generate positive 20-day returns in >60% of high-inflation observations, or the predictive power vanishes when CPIAUCSL exceeds 8.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate hikes combined with accelerating consumer price index growth create a stagflationary shock that forces immediate equity deleveraging over the following week.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by >0.25% and CPIAUCSL MoM > 0.4%
failure_condition: The combined signal of rising rates and inflation fails to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 55%, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero correlation with 60-day forward returns over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal accuracy drops below 45% in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP contracting for two consecutive quarters confirms a technical recession, triggering institutional portfolio rebalancing away from equities over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth < 0 for 2 consecutive quarters
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in >60% of confirmed recession quarters, or the recession signal fails to produce a statistically significant negative alpha.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in industrial production during a liquidity crisis indicates severe supply chain breakdowns, causing immediate negative equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines exceeding 1.0% fail to predict negative 5-day returns with a win rate above 55% during stress periods, or the signal shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.
